subject: Computer Networking
title: Network Segmentation
description: Learn Network Segmentation in one minute.

hook.text: A hacker breaches one machine. If the entire network is flat, every other machine is now reachable. Game over.

buildup.text: Segmentation divides a network into isolated zones. Each zone has its own access rules and boundaries.

discovery.text: VLANs and subnets create logical boundaries. Firewalls between segments control what traffic can cross.

twist.text: The 2013 Target breach started in their HVAC vendor's network. Without segmentation, attackers reached payment systems.

climax.text: Microsegmentation goes further — isolating individual workloads. Even within a zone, lateral movement is restricted.

punchline.text: Segmentation limits the blast radius. One breach doesn't mean total loss.

quiz.question: What does network segmentation prevent?
options: Attackers moving freely across the entire network after a breach
Users from connecting to the internet
